plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and comply with the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and broad market exposure.
Buy XLV at 8% to diversify into a high-quality healthcare sector.
Establish a 15% cash buffer for capital preservation and stability.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation with a target of ~1.5%/month, focusing on lower drawdown. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, but growth potential is limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits.
Sell SOXL entirely due to its flagged status and downtrend.
Reduce SPAL to 3% to keep leveraged ETF exposure small.
Trim SMH to 10% given its downtrend.
Buy LRCX and ASML at 7% each, leveraging strong scanner setups in chips.
Maintain a 10% cash buffer for flexibility.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets ~2.5%/month, prioritizing risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while adding high-quality tech. Expect moderate volatility and drawdowns.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single name mandate.
Sell SOXL entirely to exit the flagged position and reallocate capital.
Reduce SMH to 10% due to its downtrend.
Increase CRWD to 15% to press into a strong uptrending AI name.
Buy LRCX and ASML at 10% each, leveraging their strong scanner setups.
Buy XLK at 10% for additional broad tech exposure from a strong setup.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month by leaning into high-scoring setups and maintaining higher exposure. It carries significantly higher risk, including greater potential for drawdown and daily decay in leveraged ETFs. Strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and reallocate.
Increase QQQI to 30% as the core income allocation.
Reduce QQQ to 10% to shift from growth to income focus.
Sell SMH and CRWD to eliminate high-beta and single-name growth exposure.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to remove leveraged ETFs entirely.
Establish a 20% cash buffer for stability and liquidity.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month through steady monthly distributions, prioritizing low drawdown. While aiming for reliable income, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in selloffs. This is not a savings account.
